repo_state.notes: Test runs validated the three exposed strategies: Gj, PureSize, and MinCover.

metrics.key_question: Do native egglog join strategies preserve results on representative join shapes?
metrics.option_implication: Native planner strategies are available as correctness baselines; more instrumentation is needed to compare plan efficiency.
metrics.performance_summary: Correctness smoke tests passed; no per-strategy runtime or stage-count table was exposed by the test harness.
limitations: semantic/freshness/barrier fields are null because this lane compares planner behavior, not query semantics.
The runs validated functional equivalence on representative tests; plan-shape diffs were inspected statically in free_join/plan.rs.

strategy_notes.Gj: Classic Generic Join: one variable per stage, ordered by minimum-size variable heuristics.
strategy_notes.PureSize: Free Join: order atoms by approximate size and build fused stages from the smallest covers first.
strategy_notes.MinCover: Free Join: approximate a minimal set cover before size ordering, to reduce the number of stages.

summary.comparison_result: all strategies passed on all three representative join shapes
summary.planner_implication: Gj is the generic-join baseline; PureSize and MinCover are free-join baselines that should be compared against Gj on plan efficiency, not output correctness.
